title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
core_idea: Atomic Habits summary in 5 points (so you don't have to read all 300 pages).
bullets: You do not rise to the level of your goals, you fall to the level of your systems. Focus on the process, not the outcome.
Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement. 1% better every day = ~37x better in a year.
The 4 laws: make it obvious, make it attractive, make it easy, make it satisfying. Invert them to break a bad habit.
Identity change is the real lever. Don't aim to 'read a book', aim to 'be a reader'. Every action is a vote for the person you want to become.
Environment beats willpower. Make good habits the path of least resistance and bad ones annoying to start.
honest_take: Distilled from the top 2 Reddit threads. Enable an Anthropic API key for a synthesized, opinionated take.

quotes.text: The identity-based habits chapter is the only genuinely new idea here. Everything else is Charles Duhigg's The Power of Habit repackaged.
quotes.author: a commenter
